# Hollywood P.R. Wars & Blazy-Chanel News

Stephen A. Deal Points, Ozempic Aftershocks, Tina Brown on Trump
 ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

Deal Points, Ozempic Aftershocks, Tina Brown on Trump** | | Happy Friday and welcome back to The Daily Courant, your afternoon compilation of Puck’s best new reporting.     First up today, **Matt Belloni** unravels a lawsuit filed by the parent company of once-mighty P.R. firm PMK against a cohort of the agency’s former employees. Sure, the case underscores how the firm’s fortunes have declined over the years—but, as Matt notes, it *also* reveals how Hollywood’s P.R. landscape has radically changed.     Plus, below the fold: **Lauren Sherman** scoops the appointment of **Matthieu Blazy** at Chanel and the behind-the-scenes action that precipitated his exit from Kering. **John Ourand** decodes the *true* valve of **Stephen A. Smith** ’s soon-to-be-historic ESPN deal. And **Tara Palmeri** explains how the potential collapse of **Pete Hegseth** ’s nomination to lead the Pentagon could scuttle other **Trump** appointees’ chances, too.     Meanwhile, on the pods: **Ana Andjelic** joins Lauren on *Fashion People* to scrutinize the new Ozempic-fueled “thin is in” narrative. On *The Town* , Matt invites *Sing Sing* filmmakers **Clint Bentley** and **Greg Kwedar** on to dissect their equal pay experiment. On *Somebody’s Gotta Win* , Tara huddles up with **Chris Cillizza** and **Michael LaRosa** to evaluate **Biden** ’s legacy. On *Impolitic* , **John Heilemann** and legendary magazine editor **Tina Brown** discuss the sordid state of American politics and media. On *The Grill Room* , Dylan connects with former Hearst Magazines president **Troy Young** to probe what went wrong at *WaPo* and CNN. And on *The Powers That Be* , **Peter Hamby** and John Ourand dig into ESPN’s Stephen A. strategy. | |  | | [How to Spin the Decay of a P.R. Firm](https://email.puck.) | | MATTHEW BELLONI | | It was telling to see the parent company of what’s now called R&CPMK sue a group of defectors on Thanksgiving eve—the latest sign that the Hollywood talent P.R. business isn’t what it used to be, and neither is PMK. | | | |  | | --- | | **A MESSAGE FROM Instagram** | |  | | **Instagram Teen Accounts: a protected experience for teens, guided by parents**     Instagram Teen Accounts are designed to address parents’ biggest concerns, providing automatic protections for who can contact their teens and the content they can see.     The impact: Built-in limits give parents more peace of mind when it comes to protecting their teens.   [**Learn more**](https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dUblktZnAwcVhDTmUyZ2d3Q3NrMD0iLCJocmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9mYW1pbHljZW50ZXIubWV0YS5jb20vaW5zdGFncmFtLXNhZmV0eS8_) | | | |  | | --- | |  | | [It’s Happening: Blazy to Chanel](https://email.puck.) | | LAUREN SHERMAN | | Behind the scenes of new Chanel designer and Lagerfeld heir Matthieu Blazy’s exit from Kering, plus the unexpected leading candidate to replace him. | |  | | [Stephen A. Backs Up the Brink’s Truck](https://email.puck.) | | JOHN OURAND | | The true value of Stephen A. Smith’s soon-to-be historic ESPN deal isn’t the $20 million annual salary or the half-decade term. Rather, it’s what’s not included. | |  | | [The Hegseth Sitch & Trump Confirmation Dominoes](https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dUblktZnAwcVhDTmUyZ2d3Q3NrMD0iLCJocmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wdWNrLm5ld3MvaWYtcGV0ZS1oZWdzZXRoLXdpdGhkcmF3cy13aG8tY291bGQtYmUtbmV4dC8_) | | TARA PALMERI | | The president-elect has stopped working the phones for Pete Hegseth, and Ron DeSantis may be on deck, amid cascading scrutiny of Trump’s most controversial nominees. | | | |  | | --- | |  | |  | | | |  | | --- | |  | | [Ozempic Society & The Row’s Winning Streak](https://email.puck.) | | LAUREN SHERMAN | | Lauren is joined by fashion marketer Ana Andjelic, author of the new book, ‘Hitmakers: How Brands Influence Culture.’ They discuss how the use of GLP-1 drugs has spurred a brand new “thin is in” narrative. Then they break down what it means to be successful in the current trading environment, and exactly why brands like The Row and Abercrombie & Fitch are succeeding while others are failing. | | | |  | | --- | |  | | [Equal Pay, Equal Stake: A Filmmaking Experiment](https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dUblktZnAwcVhDTmUyZ2d3Q3NrMD0iLCJocmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wdWNrLm5ld3MvcG9kY2FzdF9lcGlzb2RlL2VxdWFsLXBheS1lcXVhbC1zdGFrZS1hLWZpbG1tYWtpbmctZXhwZXJpbWVudC8_) | | MATTHEW BELLONI | | Matt is joined by filmmakers Clint Bentley and Greg Kwedar to talk about their unique payment model behind their latest film, ‘Sing Sing.’ They discuss how they managed to pay everyone on set, from lead actor to production assistant, the exact same amount of money, while also offering equity to everyone who worked on the film. | | | |  | | --- | |  | | [What’s Left of Joe Biden’s Legacy? With Michael LaRose & Chris Cillizza](https://email.puck.) | | TARA PALMERI | | Tara is joined by political commentator Chris Cillizza and former press secretary for Jill Biden, Michael LaRosa, to analyze Joe Biden’s presidential resume as we head toward the end of his administration. They speculate on the effect Hunter Biden’s controversial pardon will have on Joe’s legacy, shine a light on Jill Biden’s influence on the president’s career, and talk about how Biden’s legislative accomplishments will be viewed in the future. | | | |  | | --- | |  | | [Tina Brown: Trump’s Extended Middle Finger, Biden’s Shambolic Exit & Martha’s Sad Denouement](https://email.puck.) | | JOHN HEILEMANN | | John is joined by Tina Brown, the legendary magazine editrix, to discuss politics and media at the end of a year of convulsive upheaval. Tina riffs on Trump’s reality show transition and its freak show appointments; the soap operatic story of Biden’s family and how it has driven him to undermine his legacy as president; the death of the magazine business; and the broader trends that have leeched the vitality and sheer fun out of journalism. | | | |  | | --- | |  | | [Troy Story](https://email.puck.) | | DYLAN BYERS | | Former Hearst Magazines president Troy Young connects with Dylan to probe what went wrong at The Washington Post and CNN, and whether Will Lewis and Mark Thompson can fix their respective companies before the media business changes again. | | | |  | | --- | |  | | [ESPN’s Stephen A. Strategy](https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dUblktZnAwcVhDTmUyZ2d3Q3NrMD0iLCJocmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wdWNrLm5ld3MvcG9kY2FzdF9lcGlzb2RlL2VzcG5zLXN0ZXBoZW4tYS1zdHJhdGVneS8_) | | PETER HAMBY & JOHN OURAND | | John Ourand joins Peter to share the latest on ESPN’s potentially historic contract negotiations with Stephen A. Smith, the larger-than-life reporter and unfiltered analyst, as he seeks to recreate himself as the face of the network. Then John and Peter weigh into the golden-at-bat controversy, and the odds that MLB throws out the rulebook to appeal to Gen Z. | | | |  | | --- | | [Puck](https://email.puck.) | | |  |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | [Facebook](https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dUblktZnAwcVhDTmUyZ2d3Q3NrMD0iLCJocmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9mYWNlYm9vay5jb20vcHVja2RvdG5ld3M_) | [Twitter](https://email.puck.) | [Instagram](https://email.puck.) | [LinkedIn](https://email.puck.) | | | |  | | --- | | Need help?
